core_mechanism: a linearized kinetic (Boltzmann) transport equation in which a streaming/source (Liouville) term drives a distribution out of equilibrium and is balanced against collision (relaxation) integrals, with a spectral gap (mass term) inserted into the dispersion relation to regulate and suppress the otherwise-divergent low-momentum (infrared) contribution
brief_reason: The abstract computes an out-of-equilibrium drag by including thermal masses consistently in both the Liouville operator and the collision integrals of a Boltzmann-equation approach, where the mass gap suppresses the source term and interaction rates and thereby tames the enhanced infrared (massless) contribution.
